mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and economic activity, which drives higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO prior value 105.22, up from 104.98 in previous month (as of t-1mo)
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns observed > 50% of the time when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE prior value 5.5% as of Sep 2020
failure_condition: Average 20-day SPX return after UNRATE <5% is negative over a 2-year period, or positive returns occur less than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and housing starts are rising strongly, it signals accommodative monetary policy stimulating housing investment, which historically leads to positive long-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and HOUST month-over-month growth above 2%
failure_condition: Over any 3-year walk-forward window, the 60-day return after the trigger is positive in less than 60% of observations, or average return is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In the early post-recession regime, rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) signal improving labor market conditions, driving positive equity returns. This direction reverses in late-cycle expansions when labor tightness leads to margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS prior value 141183k, UNRATE 5.5% as of Sep 2020
failure_condition: During identified post-recession regimes, positive 20-day returns follow rising PAYEMS in less than 50% of observations, or the signal shows negative rank IC over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS prior value $441363M as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC over 5-day horizon remains below 0.02 over any 1-year walk-forward period, or average return after RSAFS increase is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating manufacturing activity, which is a leading indicator for economic output and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Over a 2-year window, the average 20-day return following the trigger is zero or negative, or the signal produces less than 55% positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates negative real interest rates, which erodes purchasing power and signals future tightening pressure, leading to negative long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Average 60-day return after trigger is positive over any 3-year walk-forward, or negative returns occur in less than 55% of trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase following a recession, rising GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strong economic momentum, driving positive equity returns. However, in a late-cycle expansion, high GDP growth may lead to overheating and subsequent market declines.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 prior value $16,294.7B as of 2020Q2, down from earlier peaks
failure_condition: During identified early expansion regimes, positive 60-day returns follow GDP growth in less than 50% of observations, or the signal's rank IC is negative over a rolling 3-year period.
